span.p: Copilot output is generic / wrong

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Capacity is paid Fabric F2+ or Premium P1+, admin Copilot setting on, supported region?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Not a grounding problem — Copilot is unavailable/limited. Fix the capacity/admin/region gate first.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is Copilot reaching for the WRONG field/measure, or reasoning over noise IDs/duplicates?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: AI data schema — DESELECT irrelevant fields + the shadow measures so Copilot focuses on the house metric

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is there a SPECIFIC recurring question that must always return the SAME trusted visual?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Verified answer — pin the curated visual to trigger phrases
NOT on DirectQuery / local Composite; Direct Lake web-only

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Does Copilot miss BUSINESS CONTEXT — terms/goals/priorities not present anywhere in the schema?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: AI instructions — model-level free text: purpose, term to field map, the one right measure per ambiguous word

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Can consumers + Copilot not tell similar fields apart by name alone?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Descriptions + synonyms + human-readable unique names on every surviving table/column/measure

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Mark model Approved for Copilot to remove the friction treatment
